priorities.check_method: B2 phase (a) runHostTick extraction shipped (235653f). The host's ENTIRE per-tick sim body (~500 LOC) moved VERBATIM from main.ts drain loop into DOM/Pixi-free src/state/hostTick.ts runHostTick(world, deps, state) — the future worker boundary. Deliberated substitutions: !isClient conjuncts folded (provably true at call site); per-tick netTransport.peerIds() -> per-frame deps.alivePeerIds (synchronous drain, run-to-completion => equivalent; differential re-verified); closure vars -> explicit HostTickState (peerAbsentSinceTick + invariantSnap + lastViolationLogTick); in-loop screenShake.trigger at CREATURE_ATTACK -> post-drain ARC_FLASH cursor scan in main.ts (client's S31 pattern; render-identical: no render mid-drain + trigger() unconditionally REPLACES per screenShake.ts:89; cursor reset on TITLE). NONET-freeze branch + shared tail watchers (ENDGAME/music/TITLE-teardown/lastGameState) deliberately NOT moved (serve host AND client — R2/PRIME-AUDIT killed the R1 events design that would have broken client watchers). reflexion_entries_to_archive: S119-P1 #verbatim-move-plus-frozen-reference-differential: for a hot-loop extraction, transcribe the pre-refactor code INTO the test as a frozen reference and assert per-tick hash equality across forced-state scenarios — it catches transcription drift a two-run replay gate cannot (same bug on both runs), and it empirically re-verifies every deliberated substitution (per-frame alive-set) for free. PRIME-AUDIT R2 also killed an R1 events-seam design by tracing the CLIENT path: the edge watchers were shared, not host-only — trace the OTHER role's path before moving 'host' code.
